attractions.name: Alleged (Visit Ogden)
attractions.description: Alleged is a stylish three-story lounge and nightclub located in the heart of Ogden's Historic 25th Street. Housed in the renovated Keller Building—a former notorious brothel known as the Rose Rooms—the venue features a dance club on the ground floor, a sophisticated cocktail lounge on the second, and a premier rooftop bar. The establishment pays homage to its colorful past with signature cocktails named after historic local figures and design elements repurposed from the original building (Visit Ogden, Mountain Luxury) .
attractions.address: 201 Historic 25th St, Ogden, UT 84401 (Yelp, Visit Ogden)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: Closed, Thursday: 8:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Friday: 7:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Saturday: 7:00 PM - 2:00 AM, Sunday: Closed (Visit Ogden, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Visit Ogden, The Signpost)
attractions.admission: Free before 9:00 PM; cover charge typically $5.00–$10.00 after 9:00 PM or for special events (Yelp, Visit Ogden)
highlights: "Ogden's only rooftop bar offering panoramic views of the Wasatch Mountains ([Visit Ogden)", 'Three unique levels: Moxie (dance floor), The Brothel (lounge), and the Sky Bar (Mountain Luxury)', 'Historical ambiance in a repurposed 1950s brothel with original architectural details (The Signpost)', 'Signature craft cocktails named after the "madams" and "working girls" of the Rose Rooms (The Common)', 'Regular live music performances and DJ sets across different floors (Visit Ogden)', 'Eclectic and chic atmosphere that encourages a more dressed-up nightlife experience (The Signpost)']
attractions.tips: Arrive before 9:00 PM to avoid the cover charge and secure a prime spot on the rooftop for the sunset. Note that the entrance is located on the side of the building on Lincoln Avenue, up a flight of stairs. While there is no food served on-site, the central location puts it within walking distance of many of Ogden's best restaurants (Yelp, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Historic 25th Street, Union Station, Tona Sushi, Lucky Slice Pizza, Ogden Amphitheater (Tripadvisor, Visit Utah)

attractions.name: Antelope Island State Park
attractions.description: Antelope Island State Park is the largest island in the Great Salt Lake, offering a unique landscape of dry grasslands, rocky ridges, and salty waters. It is renowned for its free-ranging wildlife, including a famous bison herd, pronghorn antelope, and bighorn sheep, while providing extensive trails for hiking and cycling. (Visit Utah)
attractions.hours: Park: 6:00 am – 10:00 pm Daily; Visitor Information Center: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm; Fielding Garr Ranch: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm. (Utah State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Open Daily (Closed Thanksgiving and Christmas) (Utah State Parks)
attractions.admission: Entrance fee: $15 per vehicle (up to 8 people), Senior entrance fee (65+): $10 per vehicle, Motorcycles: $5 per motorcycle, Bicycles and Pedestrians: $3 per person. Annual Antelope Island Pass: $65. (Utah State Parks)
highlights: 'Free-roaming bison herd, one of the largest and oldest publicly owned herds in the U.S. ([Wikipedia)', 'Historic Fielding Garr Ranch, offering a glimpse into 19th-century ranching life. (Utah State Parks)', "Frary Peak, the island's highest point with 360-degree views of the Great Salt Lake. (Visit Utah)", 'International Dark Sky Park status, providing exceptional opportunities for stargazing. (Visit Utah)', 'White-sand beaches at Bridger Bay for swimming and floating in the high-salinity water. (Visit Utah)']
attractions.tips: Be prepared for biting gnats and mosquitoes, especially from April to June; wearing head nets or protective clothing is recommended. Bring plenty of water as it is scarce on the island, and remember that rideshare services do not pick up from the park. (Visit Utah, Utah State Parks)
attractions.nearby: Bear River Migratory Bird Refuge, Lagoon Amusement Park, Cherry Hills Recreation Park, and the city of Ogden. (Visit Utah)

attractions.name: Becker Brewing and Malting Company
attractions.description: Established in 1890, the Becker Brewing and Malting Company was the longest continuously operating brewery in Utah, surviving both World Wars and Prohibition. The brewery was a cornerstone of Ogden's industry for 75 years, eventually closing in 1964, but its legacy has been recently revived through authentic recreations of its historic recipes. (Utah State University, Standard-Examiner)
attractions.address: 1921 Lincoln Avenue (Historical Site) / 358 Park Boulevard (Current Taproom)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 11:00 am - 9:00 pm; Saturday: 10:30 am - 9:00 pm; Sunday: 10:30 am - 8:00 pm (Ogden Beer Company)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Ogden)
highlights: 'Longest continuously operating brewery in Utah history (75 years) ([Utah State University)', "Successfully survived Prohibition by pivoting to soft drinks, ice, and 'Becco' near-beer (Utah Stories)", "Modern relaunch of 'Becker’s Mellow Beer' in partnership with Snowbasin Resort (Snowbasin)", 'Historic site located along the banks of the Ogden River (Standard-Examiner)', "Legacy honors the Becker family's role in developing Utah's winter sports and brewing culture (KSL)", 'Extensive historical records and memorabilia preserved in the Utah State University Special Collections (Archives West)']
attractions.tips: Visit the Ogden Beer Company to see murals and relics dedicated to the original Becker brewery. Look for the 'Becker’s Mellow Beer' at Snowbasin for a taste of the original 1960s recipe recreation. (Standard-Examiner, Snowbasin)
attractions.nearby: Ogden River Parkway, Snowbasin Resort, Union Station, Historic 25th Street (Visit Ogden)

attractions.name: Ben Lomond Trail #042
attractions.description: Standing at 9,712 feet, Ben Lomond is the highest peak overlooking the Ogden area and is part of the northern Wasatch Mountains. The trail is renowned for its sweeping panoramic views of the Wasatch Range stretching from Idaho to Salt Lake City and is famously believed to be the inspiration for the Paramount Pictures logo. This popular route offers a challenging but rewarding ascent through meadows and rocky ridgelines.
attractions.hours: 24/7 for trail access; North Fork Park typically open daily from 8:00 AM to sunset (Weber County Parks)
attractions.admission: Free
attractions.phone: (385) 405-7100 (Ogden Ranger District) or (801) 399-8230 (Weber County Parks) (Forest Service, Weber County Parks)
highlights: 'Highest peak overlooking the Ogden area at 9,712 feet elevation ([Utah.com)', 'Believed inspiration for the original Paramount Pictures logo (Visit Ogden)', 'Spectacular 360-degree views of the Great Salt Lake and the Wasatch Range (Forest Service)', 'Abundant wildflower displays on the wet eastern slopes in spring and summer (Utah.com)', 'Common sightings of local wildlife such as moose along the lower sections (Utah.com)']
attractions.tips: Start your hike as early as 5:00 AM in the summer to avoid intense midday heat and exposure (Girl on a Hike). Bring at least 2-3 liters of water as there are no reliable water sources along the ridge (Girl on a Hike). Be prepared for a final mile of steep switchbacks and potential snow or mud in the early season (Forest Service).
attractions.nearby: North Fork Park, Pineview Reservoir, Ogden Canyon, Nordic Valley Ski Resort, Willard Peak (Visit Ogden, Utah.com)

attractions.name: Ben Lomond Trail #042 Forest Service
attractions.description: Ben Lomond Trail is a popular and heavily used path leading to the 9,712-foot summit of Ben Lomond Peak, offering panoramic views of the Wasatch Range. The peak was named by a Scottish immigrant for its resemblance to the Ben Lomond mountain in Scotland and is famously cited as the inspiration for the Paramount Pictures logo. The trail is used for hiking, horseback riding, and mountain biking, featuring steady ascents and a seasonal waterfall. Forest Service, Wikipedia)

attractions.admission: Free (No permits or fees required for standard hiking) Girl on a Hike, Weber County Parks & Rec
highlights: 'Scenic vistas of the Wasatch Range stretching 100 miles from the summit. [Forest Service', 'Highest peak overlooking the Ogden area at 9,712 feet. Forest Service', 'A cascading waterfall from Cold Spring located approximately 1.6 miles from the trailhead. Forest Service', 'Multi-use trail accessible to hikers, mountain bikers, and horseback riders. Forest Service', 'Inspiration for the original Paramount Pictures logo created by W.W. Hodkinson. Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Start early (by 5 am in summer) to avoid heat and potential afternoon thunderstorms; the round-trip hike can take 5–8 hours and requires significant water. Girl on a Hike, Forest Service
attractions.nearby: North Fork Park, Willard Peak, Pineview Reservoir, Causey Reservoir Weber County Parks & Rec, Forest Service

attractions.description: The Browning-Kimball Classic Car Museum features a prestigious collection of rare and pristine original automobiles from the "Golden Age" of American car craftsmanship. Established in 1981 at Ogden's Union Station, the museum showcases the evolution of automotive design through vehicles dating from 1901 to 1937. Ogden City Official Site

The facility features a large roller skating rink, multi-level laser tag, massive inflatable bounce zones, and a high-tech arcade. It is widely recognized as a top spot for birthday parties and community gatherings in the Davis and Weber County area. Classic Fun Center Layton, Facebook

attractions.name: El Monte Golf Course, Ogden City
attractions.description: Opened in 1931, El Monte Golf Course is a secluded nine-hole parkland layout situated at the mouth of the scenic Ogden Canyon. The course is renowned for its historic stone clubhouse built during the Great Depression and a pair of massive cottonwood trees standing in the middle of the first fairway. It offers a challenging par 35 experience with stunning views of the surrounding Wasatch Mountains, Ogden City.

highlights: 'Historic stone clubhouse built in 1935, listed on the National Register of Historic Places, [Ogden City', 'Signature pair of massive Fremont cottonwood trees located in the middle of the first fairway, Ogden City', 'Breathtaking views of the Wasatch Mountains and the mouth of Ogden Canyon, Visit Ogden', 'Home to the annual El Monte Four-Ball Tournament, a local tradition since 1936, Ogden City', 'Versatile 9-hole layout that doubles as a popular sledding and snow tubing destination in winter, Utah Agenda', 'Secluded atmosphere with mature trees and well-maintained greens, Tripadvisor']

attractions.name: Farmers Market Ogden (farmersmarketogden.com)
attractions.description: Farmers Market Ogden is a vibrant community gathering on Historic 25th Street that transforms three city blocks into a lively marketplace with over 300 rotating local vendors. The market celebrates Northern Utah's culture and food, offering fresh produce, handcrafted goods, live music, and family-friendly activities like community yoga (Visit Ogden, The Ogdenite). It has been recognized as the top-rated farmers market in Utah for multiple consecutive years (The Ogdenite).
attractions.address: 25th Street & Grant Ave, Ogden, UT 84401 (Visit Ogden, Kids Out and About)
attractions.hours: Summer (May 23 – Sept 12): Sat 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM; Fall (Sept 26 – Oct 17): Sat 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Winter (Jan 24 – Feb 28): Sat 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Mon-Fri & Sun: Closed (farmersmarketogden.com, Visit Ogden)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Ogden)
highlights: 'Features over 300 rotating local farmers, artisans, and food producers throughout the season ([Visit Ogden).', 'Free community yoga classes held every Saturday morning at 9:00 AM in Municipal Park (Visit Ogden).', 'Live local music performances across multiple stages, including the main stage at Lincoln Avenue (Visit Ogden).', 'Kids Curiosity Corner by the Ogden Nature Center offering educational and fun activities for children (Visit Ogden).', 'Free bike valet service available for visitors who cycle to the market (Visit Ogden).', 'Vibrant selection of locally roasted coffee, artisanal breads, and unique cultural cuisines (North Ogden Connection).']
attractions.tips: Arrive early at 8:00 AM to beat the crowds and secure the freshest produce before it sells out (Reddit). Utilize the free Ogden Express (OGX) or Trolley services for convenient transportation, or park at the Junction parking garages for easy access (Visit Ogden). Be sure to bring reusable bags for your purchases and a yoga mat if you plan to join the morning session (The Ogdenite).

attractions.name: Fort Buenaventura Park (formerly Fort Buenaventura State Park)
attractions.description: Fort Buenaventura is a reconstructed 19th-century fur-trading post located on an 84-acre tract along the Weber River. Built originally in 1846 by Miles Goodyear, the park features authentic stockade and cabin replicas that symbolize the transition from nomadic life to permanent settlement in the Great Basin (Weber County Parks, Wikipedia).

attractions.hours: 7:00 AM - 9:00 PM daily (Visit Ogden, Tripadvisor).
attractions.admission: General Entry: $1.00 - $3.00 per person; Disc Golf: $4.00 per player; Canoe Rental: ~$5.00 per hour; Group Site (Mon-Thu): $15.00 per hour; Ages 5 and under are Free (Weber County Parks, Tripadvisor, Fort Tours).
highlights: ['Reconstructed fur-trading fort with stockade and cabin replicas', '18-hole championship disc golf course (PDGA World Championship host site)', 'Canoeing pond and community fishery stocked with trout', 'Annual Mountain Man Rendezvous and Ogden Music Festival events', 'Access to the scenic Ogden River Parkway and Centennial Trail', 'Expansive off-leash dog park with river access']
attractions.tips: Pack allergy medication if visiting in spring due to heavy cottonwood seeds; check for flood alerts before visiting as the park is prone to seasonal flooding; try to visit during a Rendezvous event for the full historical experience; be aware of train noise from the nearby rail yard if camping (Tripadvisor, Airial Travel).

The park preserves 2,735 acres where visitors can see the Last Spike Site, explore original railroad grades, and view functional Victorian-era replica steam locomotives. (NPS)

attractions.description: Great Room Escape Ogden is a fully immersive and interactive escape room attraction where teams are locked in themed environments for one hour. Participants must work together to find clues, solve intricate puzzles, and complete a narrative mission before time runs out. The venue features high-quality, Hollywood-style sets and live actor involvement to create a unique "4-D" experience for families and corporate groups (Facebook, Visit Ogden, Great Room Escape FAQ)
attractions.address: 3733 S 250 W #105, Ogden, UT 84405 (Great Room Escape Ogden, Morty)
attractions.hours: Mon - Thurs: 4:30 pm – 8:30 pm; Friday: 4:30 pm – 10:00 pm; Saturday: 1:30 pm – 10:00 pm; Sunday: Closed (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Approximately $24.99 per person plus tax; weekday rates typically $22-$25, while Saturday rates range from $26-$30. Tiered group pricing is available (e.g., 2-4 players for $125, 7-8 players for $200), making larger groups more cost-effective (Alcatraz Escape Games, Official Site - Terms)
highlights: 'Themed escape rooms including "Area 51," "The Electric Chair," and "The Haunting" ([Visit Ogden)', '"Lost in the 50\'s" nostalgic adventure with high-quality set design (Morty)', 'Immersive 4-D sets featuring professional actor interaction (Great Room Escape FAQ)', 'Private booking options available for all game sessions (Official Site - Terms)', 'Corporate team-building events and large group accommodations (Great Room Escape Ogden)', 'Convenient location adjacent to the Newgate Mall with easy parking (Morty)']

Arrive at least 15 minutes early for the mandatory pre-game briefing and safety orientation. Electronic devices, including cell phones and cameras, are strictly prohibited inside the game rooms. For the best value, consider bringing a larger group to take advantage of tiered pricing structures (Alcatraz Escape Games, Great Room Escape FAQ)

This locally owned cooperative coffee shop features locally roasted beans, a variety of espresso-based drinks, and delectable treats from an in-house baker. Known for its charming loft seating and historic ambiance, it serves as a popular hub for friends, co-workers, and visitors exploring downtown Ogden.

attractions.description: Hampton Inn & Suites Ogden is a historic boutique hotel housed in the 1913 Eccles Building, a grand eight-story structure designed in the Chicago Commercial style. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the hotel features an ornate lobby with original marble craftsmanship and is directly connected to the David Eccles Conference Center. It offers guests a unique blend of early 20th-century elegance and modern amenities in the heart of downtown Ogden (Visit Ogden, Hilton).

For the best views of the city or mountains, try requesting a room on the Washington Boulevard side of the building (Tripadvisor). A free downtown trolley bus stop is conveniently located right outside the hotel (Yelp).

attractions.description: The Harvest Moon Celebration is Ogden's largest annual community festival and end-of-summer bash. Held in the heart of downtown, the event features a vibrant mix of family-friendly activities, live musical performances, local artisan vendors, and a massive beer garden showcasing Utah's craft breweries. attractions.name: Historic 25th Street
attractions.description: Historic 25th Street is a vibrant three-block historic district in Ogden, Utah, that served as a bustling hub for the Transcontinental Railroad in the late 19th century. Once known for its notorious "Two-Bit Street" reputation for gambling and saloons, it has been revitalized into a cultural center featuring preserved turn-of-the-century architecture, independently owned shops, art galleries, and restaurants. Visit Ogden, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 25th Street (between Wall Avenue and Washington Boulevard), Ogden, UT 84401-2302
attractions.hours: District is open 24/7; individual businesses typically open between 10:00 AM and 11:00 AM and close between 9:00 PM and 11:00 PM. Union Station Museums: Wed-Sat 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM. Ogden City, Evendo
attractions.admission: Free (Public Historic District); specific attractions like Union Station Museums charge $7 for adults, $5 for seniors, $4 for teens (13-17), and $3 for youth (3-12) Ogden City, WhichMuseum
highlights: 'Union Station and its four museums (Railroad, Firearms, Classic Cars, and Cowboy/Western Heritage) [Visit Utah', 'Ogden’s Farmers Market (Saturdays, June through September) Visit Utah', 'First Friday Art Stroll featuring local artist receptions and live music Ogden City', 'Historic architecture including the Bigelow-Ben Lomond Hotel Wikipedia', 'A diverse culinary scene with local favorites like Table 25 and Lucky Slice Pizza Visit Ogden']
attractions.tips: Explore on foot to best appreciate the architecture and hidden murals. Visit during the evening for a lively atmosphere with street performers and illuminated storefronts. Wear comfortable shoes and check the local events calendar for festivals like the Historic 25th Street Car Show. Evendo

attractions.name: Historic Union Stockyards Ogden (Ogden Union Stockyard Exchange Building)
attractions.description: The Historic Union Stockyards Ogden was once the largest livestock market west of Denver, operating as a major economic engine from 1917 to 1971. Today, the site has been redeveloped into the Ogden Business Exchange, a 51-acre lifestyle business park that preserves the historic 1931 Art Deco Exchange Building as its architectural centerpiece. The area integrates industrial heritage with modern recreational amenities, including public trails and river access.
attractions.hours: Sunrise to 10:00 PM (Park and trail access); Exchange Building interior is currently closed for restoration
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Historic 1931 Art Deco Exchange Building designed by Leslie S. Hodgson', 'Formerly the premier livestock trading hub in the Intermountain West', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2015', 'Integrated with the Weber River Parkway and local recreation trails', 'Centerpiece of the 51-acre Ogden Business Exchange redevelopment project', 'Proximity to outdoor amenities like the Trackline Bike Park and Ogden Kayak Park']
attractions.tips: The historic Exchange Building is currently fenced for restoration, so it is best viewed from the exterior while exploring the surrounding business park. Access the site via the Weber River Parkway trail for a scenic walk or bike ride, and look for interpretive signage that details the site's rich agricultural history. Visit the nearby Roosters B Street Brewery for a meal after your tour of the grounds.
attractions.nearby: Roosters B Street Brewery, Weber River Parkway, Trackline Bike Park, Ogden Kayak Park, Fort Buenaventura Park, Ogden's Own Distillery

attractions.name: John M. Browning Firearms Museum
attractions.description: The John M. Browning Firearms Museum showcases the life's work of the prolific firearm inventor and his descendants. Located in the historic Union Station, the museum features an extensive collection of original prototypes and production models of iconic guns like the M1911 pistol and the Browning Automatic Rifle (Ogden City official website).
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday - Tuesday: Closed (Ogden City official website)
attractions.admission: Adults (18-64): $7, Seniors (65+): $5, Teens (13-17): $4, Youth (3-12): $3, Babies (0-2): Free, Union Station Members: Free (Ogden City official website)

attractions.tips: Admission to the Firearms Museum is included with a general Union Station museum ticket, which also provides access to the railroad, car, and cowboy museums (Ogden City official website). Visitors interested in technical details should look for the 360-degree views of firearms displays (Ogden City official website).

attractions.name: Lawrence T. Dee Events Center
attractions.description: The Lawrence T. Dee Events Center is an 11,592-seat multi-purpose domed arena located on the campus of Weber State University in Ogden, Utah. Opened in 1977, it serves as the home for the Weber State Wildcats men's and women's basketball teams and is the largest arena in Utah north of Salt Lake City. The venue is a community pillar that hosts a variety of events including NCAA tournaments, concerts, and graduation ceremonies (Weber State Athletics, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Ticket Office); Closed Saturday-Sunday unless an event is scheduled (Weber State Athletics). Doors typically open 90-120 minutes before event start times.
attractions.days_open: Monday through Friday (Ticket Office); Event-dependent for arena access
attractions.admission: Tickets for basketball games generally range from $13 to $60+ (Vivid Seats, SeatGeek); Weber State students receive free or reduced admission for campus-sponsored sporting events (Weber State University). Many community events and graduations are free to the public.
highlights: 'Home to the Weber State University Wildcats men’s and women’s basketball teams ([Weber State Athletics).', 'Features a unique domed architecture with 11,592 chair-backed seats in a circular arrangement (Wikipedia).', 'Has hosted multiple NCAA Tournament games and Big Sky Conference Championships (Stadium Journey).', 'Served as a major performance venue for artists like Taylor Swift, Johnny Cash, and Imagine Dragons (The Signpost).', 'Primary host for regional high school graduation ceremonies and large-scale community gatherings (Weber School District).']
attractions.tips: Parking is generally free in the large lot surrounding the arena, but it can fill quickly during graduations or rivalry games (Weber State University). Use the free OGX Wildcat Shuttle for convenient transit to the main campus. Attendees should follow the clear bag policy, which typically limits bag size to small clutches (Vivid Seats).

attractions.name: Malan's Peak Trail
attractions.description: Malan's Peak is a prominent high point in the Wasatch Mountains overlooking Ogden, reaching an elevation of approximately 6,980 feet. The trail offers a strenuous 4.5 to 5-mile round-trip hike with over 2,000 feet of elevation gain, rewarding visitors with expansive views of the Great Salt Lake and Ogden Valley. It is named after Bartholomew "Tim" Malan, who operated a mountain resort in the adjacent Malan's Basin during the late 19th century. (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Girl on a Hike, Deseret News)
attractions.address: 2985 Buchanan Ave (29th Street Trailhead) or East end of 27th Street
attractions.region: East Bench / Wasatch Mountains (The Ogdenite, Homes.com)
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Open 24 hours for special endurance events) (Airial Travel, Find a Race)
attractions.admission: Free (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Visit Ogden)
highlights: 'Panoramic vistas of the Great Salt Lake, Antelope Island, and the Ogden urban area from the summit. ([Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Visit Ogden)', 'Rich history as the former site of the Malan Heights Hotel and resort in the 1890s. (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Katie Wanders)', 'Challenging ascent through the scenic Taylor Canyon, featuring a mix of shaded pines and open hillsides. (Visit Ogden, Road Trip Ryan)', 'Part of the TFNU Peak Challenge, which encourages hikers to bag four iconic local peaks. (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah)', "Proximity to Malan's Basin, a serene forested area perfect for a quick extension to the hike. (Deseret News, Hiiker)"]
attractions.tips: Start early to avoid direct sun exposure on the steep upper switchbacks and to secure parking. The 29th Street Trailhead is preferred for its restrooms and better parking capacity. Bring at least 2 liters of water per person, as the creek in Taylor Canyon may be dry or inaccessible later in the season. Trekking poles are highly recommended for the steep descent to protect your knees. (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Visit Ogden, Girl on a Hike)
attractions.nearby: Waterfall Canyon, Bonneville Shoreline Trail, Mount Ogden, Beus Canyon Trail, Weber State University, Historic 25th Street. (Visit Ogden, Road Trip Ryan)

attractions.name: Mount Ogden Golf Course
attractions.description: Mount Ogden Golf Course is an 18-hole championship course nestled high on the East Bench of the Wasatch Mountains, offering a unique and challenging mountain-style layout. Known for its dramatic elevation changes and pristine course conditions, it provides golfers with panoramic views of the city, valley, and surrounding peaks. The course features heavily tree-lined fairways, cold mountain streams, and strategically placed bunkers that demand accuracy and skill.
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset; Friday: Sunrise - Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset. (Tee times typically scheduled from sunrise until approximately two hours before sunset).
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday - Sunday) during the golf season (typically March 1 through November 1).
attractions.admission: Regular (Mon-Thu): $16 (9 holes) / $32 (18 holes); Regular (Fri-Sun): $17 (9 holes) / $34 (18 holes); Seniors (60+, Mon-Thu): $14 (9 holes) / $28 (18 holes); Juniors (17 & under): $10 (9 holes) / $20 (18 holes); College Students/Military (Mon-Thu): $14 (9 holes) / $28 (18 holes); Cart Fee: $10 (9 holes) / $20 (18 holes) per rider.
highlights: ['Dramatic elevation changes and unique mountain-style design.', 'Stunning panoramic views of the Wasatch Mountains and Ogden Valley.', '18-hole, par 71 layout with over 6,400 total yards.', 'Meticulously maintained greens and lush, mature tree-lined fairways.', 'Challenging multi-tiered greens that reward local knowledge.', 'Frequent wildlife sightings and a secluded, peaceful atmosphere.']
attractions.tips: Using a cart is highly recommended due to the extremely hilly terrain and significant elevation changes. First-time visitors should bring extra golf balls, as the rough and tree-lined areas can be unforgiving. Remember that putts generally break toward the west, away from the mountain peaks. Arrive early to enjoy the views and use the practice facilities before your round.

The facility houses several distinct collections, including the Utah State Railroad Museum, the John M. Browning Firearms Museum, and the Browning-Kimball Classic Car Museum (Ogden City). Visitors can explore the rich history of transcontinental rail, innovative firearm design, and vintage automobiles all under one roof (Ogden City).
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday - Tuesday: Closed (Ogden City)
attractions.admission: Adults (18-64): $7, Seniors (65+): $5, Teens (13-17): $4, Youth (3-12): $3, Babies (0-2): Free, Members: Free (Ogden City)

Browning Firearms Museum: Showcases the inventions and prototypes of the world-famous firearms designer (Ogden City).', 'Browning-Kimball Classic Car Museum: Displays a collection of beautifully restored vintage motorcars from the early 20th century (Ogden City).', 'Spencer and Hope Eccles Rail Center: An outdoor display where visitors can see massive steam and diesel locomotives up close (Ogden City).', 'Myra Powell Gallery and Gallery at the Station: Spaces dedicated to rotating art and cultural exhibitions (Ogden City).']

attractions.description: Nordic Valley Ski Resort is a family-friendly boutique ski area located in the Wasatch Mountains near Ogden, Utah. Established in 1968, it is renowned for its budget-friendly atmosphere, approachable beginner terrain, and extensive night skiing capabilities (Ski Utah, Nordic Valley Ski Resort).

attractions.description: The Val A. Browning Center for the Performing Arts is a premier cultural hub in northern Utah, hosting over 200 events annually ranging from world-class symphonies to experimental student theatre. As the home of Weber State University’s School of Performing Arts, it features three distinct venues: the 1,409-seat Austad Auditorium, the intimate Allred Theater, and the versatile Eccles Theater black box. Weber State, The Signpost

PR Newswire', 'Has hosted legendary figures such as Ronald Reagan, Neil Armstrong, and Fred Rogers. Weber State News']

attractions.name: Wasatch Roasting Company
attractions.description: Wasatch Roasting Company is a premier craft coffee roastery and specialty coffee shop located in the heart of downtown Ogden, Utah. It specializes in small-batch roasting and offers a unique multisensory experience where visitors can watch the roasting process while enjoying expertly brewed coffee in a welcoming, historic atmosphere.
attractions.hours: Monday – Saturday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Small-batch, in-house craft coffee roasting', 'Nitro cold brew and specialty espresso beverages on tap', 'Collaborative brew "Synergy Coffee Lager" with local breweries', 'Located in a historic building near Historic 25th Street', 'Outdoor seating area available for guests', 'Regular host for local art showcases and community events']
attractions.tips: Try the pour-over for a true taste of their single-origin beans, or the "Synergy" coffee lager collaboration if you're looking for a unique coffee-beer fusion. Be sure to check out the back of the shop to see the roasting equipment in action.

attractions.name: Water Lantern Festival (Davis County / Ogden)
attractions.description: The Water Lantern Festival is a family-friendly cultural event where thousands of participants gather to design and release floating lanterns onto the water. The evening features a vibrant atmosphere with live music, local food trucks, and a sunset launch that creates a stunning display of lights reflecting on the pond. It is designed to be a night of hope, reflection, and community connection.
attractions.hours: 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM (Gates open at 6:00 PM, Lantern launch begins at 9:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Children (0-7): Free; Adult Early Bird: $30.99; Adult Regular: $37.99; Adult Late: $47.99; Day of Event: $57.99; Date Night Special (2 tickets + blanket): $79.16; VIP Upgrade: $56.99
highlights: ['Personalized floating lantern launch at sunset', 'Live music performances from local and regional artists', 'Diverse selection of local food trucks and vendors', 'Interactive lawn games and a festival scavenger hunt', 'Eco-friendly, biodegradable lanterns that are fully retrieved after the event']
attractions.tips: Arrive early when gates open at 6:00 PM to secure a good spot and have plenty of time to decorate your lantern. Bring lawn chairs or blankets for seating as the event is held in a park setting. Note that this is an alcohol-free event.

attractions.name: Waterfall Canyon Trail
attractions.description: Waterfall Canyon Trail is a signature Ogden hike featuring a spectacular 200-foot waterfall cascading down a sheer cliff face. This short but strenuous 2.4-mile out-and-back trail offers panoramic views of the Ogden valley and the Great Salt Lake, transitioning from sun-exposed slopes to a lush, shady canyon. (Trails Foundation of Northern Utah, Visit Utah)
attractions.address: 29th Street Trailhead (at the east end of 29th Street), Ogden, UT 84403
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Visit Ogden); sunrise to sunset recommended (The Oaks in Ogden Canyon)
attractions.admission: Free (Girl on a Hike, The Postcard Traveler)
highlights: 'Spectacular 200-foot waterfall ([Visit Ogden)', 'Panoramic views of Ogden and the Great Salt Lake (Visit Utah)', 'Stunning frozen cascade in winter months (Girl on a Hike)', 'Connection to the historic Bonneville Shoreline Trail (Utah.com)', 'Scenic river crossings with wooden bridges (Utah Hiking Beauty)']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking shoes for the rocky scramble, bring plenty of water as the first half is unshaded, and use micro-spikes for traction during winter months. (Tripadvisor, Utah Hiking Beauty)
attractions.nearby: Mount Ogden Park, Ogden Nature Center, Ogden Botanical Gardens, Union Station, Historic 25th Street (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Weber County Fairgrounds (Golden Spike Event Center)
attractions.description: The Weber County Fairgrounds is a sprawling 141-acre multi-use complex that serves as the heart of community gatherings in Northern Utah. It features six buildings, four barns, a racetrack, a cross-country course, and extensive recreation courts, hosting everything from the annual county fair to professional rodeos and trade shows. Weber County Fair, Golden Spike Event Center

attractions.tips: Parking is free in the west dirt and north paved lots. The entire facility is smoke-free, including vapes and e-cigarettes. Pets are welcome on leashes but restricted from reserved seating events like the demolition derby. Weber County Fair

attractions.name: Weber State University
attractions.description: Weber State University is a public university located on the scenic east bench of the Wasatch Mountains in Ogden, Utah. Founded in 1889, it offers more than 225 certificate and degree programs through seven colleges and is known for its open-enrollment policy and commitment to community engagement. The campus features state-of-the-art facilities, including the Stewart Bell Tower and the Val A. Browning Center for the Performing Arts. Wikipedia, Weber State University

attractions.name: Weber State Wildcats Basketball (Dee Events Center)
attractions.description: The Weber State Wildcats basketball program is a cornerstone of Ogden sports, playing their home games at the iconic Dee Events Center. Known as the 'Purple Palace,' the 11,502-seat circular domed arena is the largest of its kind in Utah north of Salt Lake City and has a storied history of hosting NCAA Tournament games and Big Sky Championships (Wikipedia, Stadium Journey).
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Monday – Friday, 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Game times typically start at 7:00 PM for evening games or 2:00 PM for matinees (Weber State Athletics)
attractions.admission: Free for WSU students (with Wildcard and up to 4 guests); Public tickets typically range from $10 for General Admission to $35+ for Reserved seating depending on the game and tier (Weber State Athletics, The Signpost).
highlights: "Home to the Weber State Men's and Women's basketball teams since 1977 ([Weber State Sports)", "Affectionately nicknamed the 'Purple Palace' for its vibrant game-day atmosphere (Stadium Journey)", 'Circular domed architectural design with a seating capacity of approximately 11,500 (Wikipedia)', 'Boasts an impressive all-time home winning percentage of over .750 (Wikipedia)', 'Has hosted numerous NCAA Tournament rounds and Big Sky Conference tournaments (Wikipedia)', 'Serves as a multi-purpose venue for community events, graduations, and concerts (Godfrey Law)']
attractions.tips: Students should always bring their Wildcard for free admission and can often bring guests at no extra cost. Parking at the Dee Events Center is generally plentiful and free for basketball games, but arrive at least 30-45 minutes early for high-profile matchups like those against BYU or Utah State (The Signpost).
attractions.nearby: Stewart Stadium, Weber State Duck Pond, Ogden Nature Center, Historic 25th Street, Mount Ogden Park (Visit Ogden)

attractions.name: Willard Bay State Park
attractions.description: Willard Bay State Park is centered around a 9,900-acre freshwater reservoir located on the flood plains of the Great Salt Lake. The park features two marinas—North and South—offering extensive opportunities for boating, swimming, water skiing, and fishing on its warm waters. It also serves as a critical wildlife-watching area, particularly for nesting eagles during the winter months (Utah State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 900 West 650 North #A, Willard, UT 84340-9999 (Utah State Parks)
attractions.hours: 6 AM – 10 PM daily (Utah State Parks)
attractions.admission: Day-use: $25 per private vehicle; Senior Day-use: $20; Walk-in & Bicycle: $10; Camping: $60 (Full Hookup), $150 (Cabins), $200 (Cottages) (Utah State Parks Fees)
highlights: 'Freshwater boating and water skiing on the Arthur V. Watkins Dam reservoir ([Utah State Parks)', 'Year-round fishing for walleye, wiper, and channel catfish (Wikipedia)', 'Winter wildlife viewing for nesting bald eagles (Utah State Parks)', 'Full-hookup camping and modern cabins available at both North and South marinas (Utah State Parks Fees)', 'Sandy beaches for swimming and day-use picnicking (Utah State Parks)', 'State-of-the-art boat launch ramps and boat slip rentals (Utah State Parks Fees)']
attractions.tips: Boaters must certify their vessels as 'Clean, Drain, and Dry' to prevent the spread of invasive quagga mussels. All children under 13 are required to wear a life jacket while on the water. Dogs are permitted in the park but must remain on a leash and are not allowed inside cabins or glamping trailers (Utah State Parks Discover).

attractions.name: Great Salt Lake State Park
attractions.description: Great Salt Lake State Park is a state park on the south shore of the Great Salt Lake, best known for its marina and the Great Salt Lake Yacht Club. The park provides boat slips, public viewpoints, and access to the lake's unusually salty waters, which are two to seven times saltier than the ocean. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Park: Sunrise – Sunset daily; Visitor Center: 11:00 am – 7:00 pm daily Official Website
attractions.admission: Entrance fee: $10 per vehicle (up to 8 people), Senior entrance fee (65+): $5 per vehicle (up to 8 people), Bicycles and Pedestrians: $2 per person. Official Website
highlights: 'Marina with 300 boat slips and boat launching facilities [Wikipedia', 'Home to the Great Salt Lake Yacht Club, known for the "saltiest sailors on earth" Official Website', 'Spectacular viewpoints of the Great Salt Lake and nearby Antelope Island Official Website', 'Major stop for millions of migratory birds, making it a popular destination for bird watching Official Website', 'Year-round campground with RV sites and primitive beach tent-only sites Official Website', 'Access to Black Rock via a newly added trailhead Official Website']
attractions.tips: Check water levels before visiting as they can vary significantly. Bring bug spray if visiting in early spring or fall to avoid midges. Commissioned by brothers David and Joseph Peery during Utah's mining boom, the three-story building features early Prairie and Classical Revival architecture and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
